footer.tagline: Unique boat experiences in the Egadi Islands

about.title: About Us
about.subtitle: A passion for the sea, since 2010
about.heroText: Egadisailing was born from a passion for the sea and a love for the Egadi Islands. Since 2010, we offer unique boat experiences for those who want to discover one of the most beautiful stretches of sea in the Mediterranean.
about.storyText: It all started with a boat and a dream: to let every guest experience the magic of the Egadi as we live it every day. Today, with a curated fleet and a passionate crew, we continue to turn every outing into an unforgettable memory.
about.value1Desc: The sea is our home. Every day on the boat is an opportunity to share the beauty of the Egadi with our guests.
about.value2Desc: Warm welcome, lovingly prepared lunches and attention to detail: Sicilian tradition aboard our boats.
about.value3Desc: We sail responsibly, protecting the marine ecosystem of the Egadi for future generations.
about.crewText: Our team is made up of sea professionals: experienced skippers, talented chefs, and attentive hostesses. Every crew member shares the same passion for the Egadi and for hospitality.

islands.title: The Egadi Islands
islands.subtitle: Three jewels in the heart of the Mediterranean

favignana.name: Favignana
favignana.description: The largest of the Egadi, famous for Cala Rossa and its turquoise waters. Known as the 'Butterfly on the Sea' for its distinctive shape, Favignana offers stunning beaches, a charming town centre and the former Florio Establishment, a testament to the historic tuna fishing tradition.
favignana.highlights: Cala Rossa, Cala Azzurra, Lido Burrone, Former Florio Establishment

levanzo.name: Levanzo
levanzo.description: The smallest and wildest, home to the Grotta del Genovese. With just 200 inhabitants, Levanzo is an unspoiled gem where time seems to have stood still. Its crystal-clear waters and rich marine life make it perfect for snorkelling.
levanzo.highlights: Grotta del Genovese, Cala Minnola, Cala Fredda, Faraglione

marettimo.name: Marettimo
marettimo.description: The most remote and unspoiled, a snorkelling paradise. Marettimo is the wildest island of the archipelago, with hiking trails through lush nature and sea caves accessible only from the water.
marettimo.highlights: Grotta del Cammello, Punta Troia, Cala Bianca, Scalo Maestro

boats.title: Our Boats
boats.subtitle: Comfort and safety for your sea adventures

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know before setting sail
faq.a1: Sunscreen (biodegradable preferably), swimsuit, personal towel, sun hat and comfortable shoes. We also recommend bringing a light sweater for the return trip.
faq.a2: Yes. Up to 15 days before departure you receive a full refund; from 14 to 7 days before departure 50% is refunded; from 6 days to departure cancellations are non-refundable. Date changes are always free when the new date is available.
faq.q3: What happens in case of bad weather?
faq.a3: We constantly monitor weather conditions. In case of bad weather, we will contact you to reschedule the trip or provide a full refund. The safety of our guests is our top priority.
faq.a4: Yes, children are welcome on board! For very young children (under 3 years old), please contact us in advance. Children's life jackets are always available.
faq.a5: For Social Boating and Exclusive Experience, the chef-prepared lunch is included in the price. For Boat Tours, it is possible to add the lunch service upon request.
faq.a6: Boarding takes place at the Port of Trapani. We will send you exact directions and the meeting point by email after booking confirmation.
